sources.name: Adventure
sources.rationale: Cross-platform component, audience, dialog, resource-pack, serializer, and NBT APIs.

sources.name: Adventure Platform
sources.rationale: Archived legacy platform adapters retained for historical routing only.

sources.name: Adventure Platform Mod
sources.rationale: Near-native Adventure integration for version-pinned Fabric and NeoForge clients.

sources.name: Animated Java
sources.rationale: Blockbench exporter for display-entity animation and generated data/resource packs.

sources.name: AuraSkills
sources.rationale: Skills, stats, mana, abilities, XP, modifiers, rewards, profiles, events, and progression APIs.

sources.name: AuthMeReloaded
sources.rationale: Authentication/session, hash strategy, events, migration, command guards, proxy, and storage contracts.

sources.name: AutoRenamingTool
sources.rationale: Extensible JVM renaming and remapping tooling used in the NeoForge ecosystem.

sources.name: AzureLib
sources.rationale: Loader-specific Bedrock-style animation engine with a distinct GeckoLib-derived API.

sources.name: beet
sources.rationale: Python development and deterministic build toolkit for data and resource packs.

sources.name: BentoBox
sources.rationale: Addon lifecycle, islands, worlds, hooks, ranks, challenges, commands, persistence, and events.

sources.name: BetonQuest
sources.rationale: Declarative conversations, conditions, events, objectives, variables, packages, journals, and extension APIs.

sources.name: Blockbench
sources.rationale: Canonical low-poly model, animation, codec, format, and plugin authoring application.

sources.name: Blockbench Plugins Registry
sources.rationale: Official executable plugin registry; every plugin requires separate license and supply-chain review.

sources.name: BlueMap
sources.rationale: Map, marker, world/render configuration, asset/web extension, storage, and live-update APIs.

sources.name: Brigadier
sources.rationale: Mojang command parser used by Minecraft and many platform command APIs.

sources.name: Bukkit Legacy
sources.rationale: Historical Bukkit API identity; current Bukkit-derived facts come from maintained Spigot/Paper sources.

sources.name: BungeeCord
sources.rationale: Canonical proxy and plugin API with a custom license and security-sensitive forwarding model.

sources.name: Canvas
sources.rationale: Historical Fabric shader renderer and resource-pack material pipeline.

sources.name: Citizens
sources.rationale: NPC registries, traits, navigation, goals, metadata, speech, lifecycle, events, and persistence.

sources.name: cloud
sources.rationale: Typed command managers, parsers, contexts, suggestions, annotations, coordinators, and platform adapters.

sources.name: Codebook
sources.rationale: PaperMC remapping pipeline tooling; capability metadata is safer than source mirroring.

sources.name: CommandAPI
sources.rationale: Brigadier trees, native arguments, executors, suggestions, permissions, conversion, and registration lifecycle.

sources.name: CraftEngine
sources.rationale: Config-driven custom items, blocks, recipes, packs, protocol integration, and unstable NMS helpers.

sources.name: DataFixerUpper
sources.rationale: Mojang typed data migration framework; actual embedded versions remain game-version-specific.

sources.name: DecentHolograms
sources.rationale: Hologram/page/line, viewer, placeholder, animation, click action, persistence, and projection APIs.

sources.name: Denizen
sources.rationale: Object/tag model, script containers, commands, events, queues, definitions, and Citizens integration.

sources.name: Dynmap
sources.rationale: Stable map and marker APIs, tiles, render components, web events, and platform adapters.

sources.name: Embeddium
sources.rationale: Sodium-derived renderer focused on NeoForge and mod compatibility.

sources.name: Entity Model Features
sources.rationale: Client implementation of OptiFine-style custom entity model features.

sources.name: Entity Texture Features
sources.rationale: Client random, emissive, blinking, and conditional entity texture features.

sources.name: EssentialsX
sources.rationale: User state, homes, warps, kits, teleport, economy, commands, spawn/chat integrations, and events.

sources.name: Fabric API
sources.rationale: Canonical modular Fabric events, registries, networking, resources, models, and rendering hooks.

sources.name: Fabric Documentation
sources.rationale: Versioned official Fabric guidance; noncommercial ShareAlike terms require summary-first ingestion.

sources.name: Fabric Intermediary
sources.rationale: Archived historical stable namespace; current metadata is resolved through Fabric Meta.

sources.name: Fabric Loader
sources.rationale: Canonical Fabric mod loader, entrypoint, metadata, and mapping resolver APIs.

sources.name: Fabric Loom
sources.rationale: Gradle development, source mapping, run configuration, and remapping tooling.

sources.name: mapping-io
sources.rationale: Mapping format reader, writer, tree, and conversion APIs.

sources.name: Fabric Meta
sources.rationale: Canonical Fabric service source behind version, loader, installer, and mapping metadata.

sources.name: Tiny Remapper
sources.rationale: High-performance Tiny namespace bytecode remapper used by Fabric tooling.

sources.name: Yarn
sources.rationale: Historical human-readable mappings through the obfuscation era; not a 26.1+ requirement.

sources.name: FastAsyncWorldEdit
sources.rationale: Parallel edit queues, extents, chunks, history, memory controls, and WorldEdit compatibility seams.

sources.name: Folia
sources.rationale: Region-threaded Paper fork whose scheduler and ownership model requires explicit compatibility.

sources.name: MinecraftForge
sources.rationale: Canonical Forge/FML loader and mod API; exact Minecraft-Forge artifact pairs are mandatory.

sources.name: Forge Documentation
sources.rationale: Versioned Forge development guidance, including client model and geometry-loader APIs.

sources.name: ForgeGradle
sources.rationale: Forge Gradle development, mapping, patching, and reobfuscation tooling.

sources.name: GeckoLib
sources.rationale: Loader-specific skeletal animation and rendering API for entities, items, and blocks.

sources.name: Geyser
sources.rationale: Java-to-Bedrock bridge, pack translation, extensions, and a separate client capability boundary.

sources.name: Grim
sources.rationale: Source for original synthesis of packet order, prediction, latency, collision replication, and check pipelines.

sources.name: Iris
sources.rationale: Shader-pack loader with public APIs and a tight exact-version relationship to Sodium.

sources.name: Iris ShaderDoc
sources.rationale: Historical shader-pack pipeline terminology; current behavior requires artifact testing.

sources.name: ItemsAdder API
sources.rationale: Public API names and lifecycle docs for a proprietary custom-content and pack plugin.

sources.name: Lorenz
sources.rationale: Quiet but useful historical JVM mapping model library.

sources.name: LuckPerms
sources.rationale: Permissions provider, users, groups, nodes, tracks, contexts, query options, caches, and messaging.

sources.name: MCProtocolLib
sources.rationale: Stateful Java sessions, packets, encryption, compression, authentication, and codecs.

sources.name: mcmeta
sources.rationale: Processed versioned Minecraft reports and assets used only as a corroborating change index.

sources.name: mcMMO
sources.rationale: Skill, XP, profile, ability, party, combat/gathering event, modifier, and persistence APIs.

sources.name: minecraft-data
sources.rationale: Language-neutral Minecraft data and protocol schemas with mixed per-file provenance.

sources.name: Minestom
sources.rationale: From-scratch extensible server library and protocol implementation with current-version-first support.

sources.name: Mixin
sources.rationale: ASM mixin framework; injection contracts remain exact-target-bytecode-specific.

sources.name: MockBukkit
sources.rationale: Plugin test lifecycle, server/player/world mocks, scheduler/event simulation, and assertions.

sources.name: Multiverse-Core
sources.rationale: World lifecycle, generators, properties, destinations, access control, events, and commands.

sources.name: NeoForge
sources.rationale: Canonical NeoForge loader and API for events, registries, data, networking, models, and resources.

sources.name: NeoForge Documentation
sources.rationale: Official versioned NeoForge development documentation.

sources.name: NeoForm
sources.rationale: Minecraft source/form transformation configuration; generated contents remain license-sensitive.

sources.name: Nexo Documentation
sources.rationale: Public lifecycle, API, version, generated-pack, item, block, and furniture docs for a commercial plugin.

sources.name: node-minecraft-protocol
sources.rationale: Node Java-protocol parser, serializer, session, encryption, authentication, and test ecosystem.

sources.name: Nova
sources.rationale: Custom items, blocks, tile entities, attachments, behaviors, models, packs, GUIs, storage, and addon APIs.

sources.name: Oraxen
sources.rationale: Metadata for custom items, glyphs, models, packs, mechanics, events, and compatibility under a restrictive custom license.

sources.name: PackSquash
sources.rationale: Resource/data-pack optimizer whose transformed output must be validated per target.

sources.name: Paper
sources.rationale: Canonical Paper server/API, fork lineage, schedulers, displays, dialogs, registries, and components.

sources.name: Paper Documentation
sources.rationale: Official Paper, Folia, Velocity, Adventure, display, dialog, pack, and userdev guidance.

sources.name: paperweight
sources.rationale: Supported Paper build/userdev workflow; implementation license requires cautious ingestion.

sources.name: Parchment
sources.rationale: Historical parameter and documentation enrichment layered over official mappings.

sources.name: PlaceholderAPI
sources.rationale: Expansion lifecycle, placeholder parsing, relational placeholders, caching, and service discovery.

sources.name: minecraftPlayerAnimator
sources.rationale: Client player pose/animation layer with sensitive renderer-mixin compatibility.

sources.name: Polymer
sources.rationale: Fabric server-side custom content, generated packs, registry projection, and virtual entities for vanilla clients.

sources.name: ProtocolLib
sources.rationale: Mature Bukkit/Paper packet event, container, structure, send, and receive APIs.

sources.name: ProtoDef
sources.rationale: Declarative protocol schema language used by Prismarine tooling; quiet and non-authoritative.

sources.name: Purpur
sources.rationale: Configurable Paper fork with additional APIs and composite inherited licensing.

sources.name: Quests
sources.rationale: Quest stages, objectives, requirements, rewards, conversations, modules, events, and persistence.

sources.name: Reflection Remapper
sources.rationale: Mapping-aware reflection helper for Paper Mojang-runtime lines.

sources.name: SkinsRestorer
sources.rationale: Skin data/signature, player apply/update, proxy messaging, storage, lifecycle, and platform abstraction.

sources.name: Skript
sources.rationale: Syntax registration, parsing, events, conditions, effects, expressions, types, and addon lifecycle.

sources.name: Slimefun 4
sources.rationale: Custom items, research, recipes, machines, energy, block menus, handlers, addons, and persistence.

sources.name: Sodium
sources.rationale: High-performance renderer replacement under PolyForm Shield; metadata/public API links only.

sources.name: Sponge
sources.rationale: Concrete Sponge implementations whose tags and game targets differ from API branches.

sources.name: SpongeAPI
sources.rationale: Canonical plugin API independent of a concrete Sponge runtime/game target.

sources.name: SpongeDocs
sources.rationale: Official docs retained with freshness warnings when they lag implementation release metadata.

sources.name: Spyglass
sources.rationale: Language-server and editor tooling for Java data and resource packs.

sources.name: SRGUtils
sources.rationale: SRG, TSRG, Tiny, and mapping model utilities.

sources.name: Towny
sources.rationale: Metadata for residents, towns, nations, plots, permissions, economy, siege, and events under restrictive terms.

sources.name: Vault
sources.rationale: Legacy economy, permission, and chat provider contracts retained as an interoperability baseline.

sources.name: Veil
sources.rationale: Advanced mod rendering framework for shaders, framebuffers, post processing, and animation.

sources.name: Velocity
sources.rationale: Modern proxy API, events, forwarding, plugin messaging, and backend topology.

sources.name: ViaBackwards
sources.rationale: Directed older-client to newer-server translation with explicit lossy feature boundaries.

sources.name: Via Mappings
sources.rationale: Via-specific mapping generator and adjacent-protocol data, not a general Mojang mapping source.

sources.name: ViaRewind
sources.rationale: Legacy 1.7/1.8 translation addon requiring ViaVersion and ViaBackwards.

sources.name: ViaVersion
sources.rationale: Directed newer-client to older-server protocol translation and public API.

sources.name: FREX
sources.rationale: Historical experimental rendering-extension and material API underlying Canvas.

sources.name: Waterfall
sources.rationale: Historical BungeeCord fork retained for discovery; prefer Velocity or canonical Bungee evidence.

sources.name: WorldEdit
sources.rationale: Edit sessions, extents, operations, regions, clipboards, schematics, masks, patterns, and history.

sources.name: WorldGuard
sources.rationale: Region managers, protected regions, flags, query/cache, sessions, events, and custom flag registration.

sources.name: XSeries
sources.rationale: Version-aware material, sound, particle, potion, profile, reflection, serialization, and capability helpers.
